title: Carpotroche ramosii
intro: Carpotroche ramosii, also known as the Mexican copal tree, is a medicinal plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The health benefits of Carpotroche ramosii include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Carpotroche ramosii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Carpotroche ramosii is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or resin extract, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including coughs, colds, and respiratory infections.
